text: In this video we demonstrate a novel method of knot tying during robot assisted laparoscopic surgery.
This is an ideal technique for placing efficient and secure knots with short suture lengths.
The surgeon uses the left needle driver to grasp the right tail of the suture, preparing for knot tying during robot-assisted laparoscopic surgery.
The surgeon overlays the right tail of the suture on top of the left tail to create a visible loop during robot-assisted laparoscopic surgery.
The right needle driver is maneuvered through the loop to grasp the right tail of the suture, demonstrating a step in the knot tying technique during robot-assisted laparoscopic surgery.

The surgeon releases the right tail of the suture using the left needle driver and then grasps the left suture, demonstrating precise manipulation of suture tails during robotic-assisted laparoscopic surgery.
The surgeon uses both needle drivers to pull the sutures apart, visibly tightening the knot.
The surgeon alternates between using the right and left threads to create loops, demonstrating the repetitive technique of knot tying with visible suture manipulation.
